WEV evalConst models P1 store y=r0+1 as constant 1 (loaded value->0), so a y=1 write exists and the wired candidate is consistent => ALLOWED; Dat3M computes real values: y=1 requires P1 read x=0, contradicting exists 1:r0=1 => FORBIDDEN (matches JMM). See eval/tc06-analysis.md.

reverse case: WEV wires a single co order (init,y=1,y=2) making the two reads r0=2,r1=1 a CoRR violation => FORBIDDEN; the outcome is consistent under co (init,y=2,y=1), which Dat3M finds (co is existential) => ALLOWED. WEV single-candidate co limitation (cf. eval/co-enumeration-results.csv).
WEV: no write of value 2 to y/z (data-dep stores modeled as 0), reads default to init => degenerate ALLOWED; Dat3M propagates concrete values through x->y->z->x and forbids the thin-air chain.
